en_body:
Night blue as a flower not yet opened
Who walks, stirring the deep-wet dew
Or I alone awaken from sleep
Thinking she crossed over to visit my dream.

Skin white as a pair of ivory chopsticks
Within her house she raises a White-Crane
Night after night it perches on the moon-roof
Shining down into the colorless clear pond...
I look and imagine her appearing
Gently releasing flowers upon the water.

She is a daughter of Heaven
reincarnated into a human life
At her house flowers fall, birds sing
Morning pours sunlight around all seasons.
Passing by I often steal a glance
secretly gather sun-colors home to nurture
inside the lonely garden of this mortal world.

She lives in a house with a gate
Two Cranes stand upon either side
Within are white Frangipani flowers
serenely fragrant through all four seasons...
She often stands inside the purple doorway
Wearing flowered silk, gazing upon Life;
I walk the road of red dust
once in a while crane my neck to look.

She is the daughter within the tower
Fond of showing soft gowns and white skin
The world believes she is a fairy
I love her ivory skin, her crimson lips.

Many times I remind myself:
She is only a girl,
Only a beautiful girl!

(Williamsburg, Iowa, 1977)
note: The White Crane and the daughter of Heaven evoke Taoist immortals, while reincarnation belongs to Buddhist cosmology.
